rag_rules: When fixing type mismatches in AGREE contracts, ensure left and right sides of binary expressions have matching types.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
generic_rules: AGREE type declarations must use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid scalar type names.
In AGREE contracts, guarantee expressions must relate visible inputs to visible outputs without referencing implementation-only paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks and must align with AADL scope and type definitions.
Assign statements in implementation contracts must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Do not write guarantee statements in implementation scope that reference subcomponent paths not visible at the component type level.
